hero.headline: O.P. Jindal Global University 1-Year Online MBA in Supply Chain & Operations Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: India's only 1-year fully online MBA. AACSB accredited. Institution of Eminence (IoE). 72 credits in 12 months. QS 5 Star rated.

sections.tldr: JGU's 1-Year Online MBA in Supply Chain & Operations Management covers Operations Research, Global Logistics, Project Management Analytics, and Service Operations Management across 72 credits compressed into 12 months. AACSB accredited (held by ~6% of B-schools globally) and backed by IoE status from the Ministry of Education. Built for supply chain and operations professionals with 3-8 years of experience in logistics, procurement, manufacturing operations, or service delivery who want an internationally benchmarked MBA in one year.

about.body:
O.P. Jindal Global University (JGU) is one of only four private universities in India to hold Institution of Eminence (IoE) status, declared by the Ministry of Education under UGC (Institutions of Eminence Deemed to be Universities) Regulations, 2017. This IoE designation grants JGU the autonomy to design innovative programmes, including India's only 1-year fully online MBA.

The Supply Chain & Operations Management specialisation pairs a rigorous management core (Strategic Management, Financial Management, Business Analytics, Marketing, Operations Management, Leadership) with four elective subjects: Operations Research, Global Logistics, Project Management Analytics, and Service Operations Management. The programme runs 72 credits in 12 months, the same credit load as conventional 2-year MBA programmes compressed into an accelerated format.

JGU holds AACSB accreditation, a global quality standard met by only about 6% of business schools worldwide and roughly 1% in India. The university is also QS 5 Star rated and UGC recognised. The Supply Chain & Operations MBA targets professionals in procurement, logistics, manufacturing planning, warehouse management, or service delivery who want management credentials that match the global scale of modern supply chains. Full programme details at /universities/op-jindal-global-university-online/mba.

subjects.name: The Firm and the Consumer
subjects.description: Analyse microeconomic foundations of firm behaviour, consumer demand theory, pricing strategy, and market structures relevant to managerial decision-making.

subjects.name: Global Economics
subjects.description: Examine macroeconomic indicators, international trade theory, exchange rate dynamics, and fiscal and monetary policy impacts on global business operations.

subjects.name: Business Statistics
subjects.description: Apply probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and statistical inference to business data for evidence-based decision-making.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.description: Cover capital budgeting, cost of capital estimation, working capital management, dividend policy, and corporate financing decisions.

subjects.name: Marketing Management
subjects.description: Study market segmentation, product positioning, pricing frameworks, distribution channel design, and integrated marketing communication strategies.

subjects.name: Organisational Behaviour
subjects.description: Explore individual motivation theories, group dynamics, leadership models, organisational culture, and change management approaches in modern workplaces.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.description: Learn process design, capacity planning, inventory control, quality management, and lean operations principles for service and manufacturing contexts.

subjects.name: Business Analytics
subjects.description: Apply data visualisation, predictive modelling, and prescriptive analytics tools to transform business data into actionable management insights.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.description: Develop competitive strategy using industry analysis, resource-based views, diversification frameworks, and strategic execution plans for organisations.

subjects.name: Leadership
subjects.description: Build leadership capabilities through self-awareness frameworks, influence strategies, team management practices, and ethical leadership in organisational contexts.

subjects.name: Operations Research
subjects.description: Apply linear programming, transportation models, assignment problems, queuing theory, and simulation techniques to optimise supply chain and operations decisions.

subjects.name: Global Logistics
subjects.description: Design global logistics networks covering freight management, customs and trade compliance, warehouse strategy, last-mile delivery, and cross-border supply chain coordination.

subjects.name: Project Management Analytics
subjects.description: Use data-driven project planning with earned value analysis, risk quantification, schedule optimisation, and resource allocation for complex operations projects.

subjects.name: Service Operations Management
subjects.description: Manage service delivery systems including capacity planning for services, service quality measurement, demand forecasting, and service process redesign.

syllabus.note: JGU's 1-year MBA covers 72 credits in 12 months. Core subjects span the first two trimesters. Specialisation electives are chosen in the final trimester alongside a capstone project.

whoHires.body:
Supply Chain & Operations MBA graduates from JGU's 1-year programme target lateral career advancement in these sectors:

**Industries:** E-commerce logistics, Manufacturing, FMCG supply chain, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Third-party logistics (3PL), Consulting (operations advisory), Retail

**Mid-level roles (typical for 1-year MBA candidates):** Supply Chain Manager, Operations Manager, Logistics Head, Procurement Manager, Demand Planning Lead, Project Manager (operations)

**Senior roles (5-8+ years experience):** VP Supply Chain, Head of Operations, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director of Logistics, Head of Procurement, VP Manufacturing Operations

**Target organisations:** E-commerce and quick-commerce firms, FMCG multinationals, automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers, 3PL providers, consulting firms with operations advisory practices, and manufacturing conglomerates.

Note: JGU's 1-year MBA is designed for working professionals seeking career acceleration, not entry-level placement. The programme does not offer campus placements.

skills.body:
The JGU Supply Chain & Operations specialisation builds skills that COOs and supply chain heads test during lateral hiring:

**Technical skills:**
- Operations Research: Apply linear programming and simulation to optimise inventory levels, transportation routes, production schedules, and warehouse layouts
- Global Logistics: Design end-to-end logistics networks, manage freight across modes, handle customs compliance, and coordinate cross-border supply chains with trade regulation awareness
- Project Management Analytics: Use earned value management, Monte Carlo simulation for risk, critical path analysis, and data-driven resource allocation for large operations projects
- Service Operations: Design service delivery systems, forecast demand for service capacity, measure service quality using structured frameworks, and redesign processes for efficiency

**Strategic skills:**
- Supply Chain Strategy: Align supply chain design with corporate strategy, make insource vs outsource decisions, and design resilient supply networks for disruption scenarios
- Cost Optimisation: Identify and eliminate waste using lean principles, right-size inventory, and build total cost of ownership models for procurement decisions

**Leadership skills:** Lead cross-functional supply chain transformation projects, manage supplier and 3PL relationships, present operations metrics to boards, and build operational excellence culture in teams.

comparisons.body:
JGU is a premium-tier 1-year MBA. Comparisons reflect this positioning.

**vs MAHE Online MBA (Rs 2,92,000, 2-year, NIRF #3):** MAHE offers NIRF #3 ranking and strong operations management faculty. JGU offers AACSB accreditation (global gold standard), IoE status, and completion in 12 months instead of 24. For supply chain professionals managing live logistics networks, taking two years is a significant opportunity cost. JGU's Global Logistics and Operations Research electives address global supply chain complexity. MAHE's NIRF ranking carries strong weight with Indian manufacturing employers.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA (Rs 1,96,000-2,20,000, 2-year):** NMIMS offers Operations and Data Sciences Management as a combined specialisation. JGU's Supply Chain & Operations Management is more focused on logistics, operations research, and service operations. For professionals specifically targeting supply chain leadership roles, JGU's dedicated specialisation and 1-year format offer a more targeted path. NMIMS serves professionals wanting a data science and operations combination.

**vs Amity Online MBA (Rs 2,25,000, 2-year, NIRF #22):** Amity offers Production and Operations Management at accessible fees with NIRF #22 ranking. JGU targets a premium segment with AACSB, IoE status, and the 1-year accelerated format. For experienced supply chain professionals (5+ years) managing global or multi-site operations, JGU's Global Logistics content and AACSB credential add international weight.

fees.body:
O.P. Jindal Global University 1-Year Online MBA in Supply Chain & Operations Management fee structure is available upon application. EMI starting at Rs 6,319 per month directly through the university.

JGU is an Institution of Eminence declared by the Ministry of Education, AACSB accredited (global gold standard held by ~6% of B-schools worldwide), and QS 5 Star rated. The 1-year format (12 months, 72 credits) is enabled by JGU's IoE status under UGC Regulations 2017.

Contact our counsellor for current fee structure, payment options, and EMI tenure. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JGU each quarter. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The O.P. Jindal Global University MBA degree certificate carries the JGU name, the MBA designation, and identifies the Supply Chain & Operations Management specialisation. JGU is UGC recognised, holds Institution of Eminence status from the Ministry of Education, and is AACSB accredited. The AACSB accreditation is recognised globally and signals that JGU's business programmes meet international quality benchmarks. The degree is valid for employment, promotions, higher studies, and international credential evaluation. For supply chain professionals operating across borders, the AACSB and IoE credentials carry weight with global employers.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from JGU 1-Year Online MBA Supply Chain & Operations Management learners. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Rajesh Kumar
items.liked: Global Logistics module covered cross-border supply chain coordination that I apply daily in my automotive supply chain role.
items.disliked: Fee information is not disclosed upfront on the website. Had to contact admissions for exact numbers.
items.body: I manage inbound logistics for an automotive OEM with 7 years of experience. The 1-year format was the deciding factor. I could not leave active supply chain operations for two years. The Global Logistics module covered freight management, customs compliance, and multi-country coordination that I use in my daily work. Operations Research gave me optimisation frameworks for our warehouse network redesign. The AACSB credential was recognised by our Japanese parent company's global procurement team. Promoted to Head of Logistics within 4 months of completing the degree.

items.name: Ananya Singh
items.liked: Project Management Analytics was the standout. Earned value analysis and risk quantification improved how I manage multi-crore operations projects.
items.disliked: Service Operations module is less relevant for manufacturing professionals. Would prefer a manufacturing-specific elective option.
items.body: I lead operations projects at a pharmaceutical company with 6 years of experience. The Project Management Analytics elective directly improved my project governance. I now use earned value management and Monte Carlo simulation for risk assessment on every major project. The core subjects, especially Financial Management, helped me build better business cases for capex proposals. The AACSB tag differentiates this degree from other online MBAs in conversations with global pharma firms. The 12-month intensity is manageable if you plan your weeks around project deadlines.

items.name: Vikash Jain
items.liked: Operations Research gave me quantitative tools I was missing. IoE status and AACSB accreditation are genuine credential differentiators.
items.disliked: No campus placement support. Expected for working professionals, but the university should state this clearly during admission.
items.body: I was a procurement manager at an FMCG company for 5 years. Wanted an MBA to move from procurement into broader supply chain leadership. Operations Research and Global Logistics together gave me the quantitative and strategic toolkit for supply chain planning roles. The core MBA subjects (Strategy, Analytics, Marketing) built cross-functional understanding that procurement specialists often lack. Applied for a Supply Chain Director role at a competitor and got shortlisted. The AACSB and IoE credentials were specifically mentioned as positive factors in the interview.

faqs.question: How can JGU offer a 1-year MBA?
faqs.answer: JGU holds Institution of Eminence (IoE) status under UGC (Institutions of Eminence Deemed to be Universities) Regulations, 2017. IoE status grants autonomy for innovative programme design, including accelerated formats. The 1-year MBA covers 72 credits, the same credit load as conventional 2-year MBA programmes, compressed into 12 months. Only four private universities in India hold IoE status.

faqs.question: What is AACSB accreditation?
faqs.answer: AACSB (Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business) is the global gold standard for business school accreditation. Only about 6% of business schools worldwide hold AACSB accreditation, and roughly 1% in India. It signals that the institution meets rigorous international benchmarks for curriculum, faculty qualifications, and learning outcomes. AACSB-accredited degrees are recognised by global employers and universities for further studies.

faqs.question: Is JGU's 1-year MBA valid?
faqs.answer: Yes. JGU is UGC recognised and holds IoE status from the Ministry of Education. The programme is AACSB accredited and covers 72 credits, meeting UGC norms. The degree is valid for employment, promotions, and higher studies in India and internationally.

faqs.question: Does this MBA prepare for supply chain certifications like CSCP or CPIM?
faqs.answer: The Operations Research and Global Logistics electives cover foundational concepts that overlap with APICS CSCP and CPIM exam content. The MBA provides management breadth and supply chain strategy. For certification-specific exam preparation, you would need to study the APICS body of knowledge separately. The MBA and CSCP or CPIM together create a strong supply chain leadership profile.

faqs.question: Is the programme suitable for professionals in e-commerce and quick-commerce logistics?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Global Logistics module covers last-mile delivery, warehouse strategy, and cross-border coordination. Service Operations Management addresses demand forecasting and capacity planning for high-velocity service environments. These are directly applicable to e-commerce and quick-commerce operations. The Project Management Analytics elective helps manage technology and infrastructure rollout projects common in this sector.

chooseIf: You are a supply chain or operations professional with 3-8 years of experience who wants an MBA completed in 12 months, not 24, without compromising on credential quality.
You manage global or multi-site supply chains and value AACSB accreditation for international employer recognition.
You want a premium MBA credential with dedicated supply chain electives (Operations Research, Global Logistics) rather than a general operations management track.
consider: You are early in your career (0-2 years) and need campus placement support. JGU's 1-year MBA is designed for working professionals, not entry-level hiring pipelines.
You want deep ERP system training (SAP, Oracle) or hands-on warehouse management system skills. This MBA builds strategic supply chain thinking, not software proficiency.
You want the lowest possible fee for an operations MBA. JGU is premium-priced. Consider IGNOU or LPU for budget-optimised options.
